business.headline: Forgotten fortune: Why assets worth Rs 2 lakh crore remain unclaimed in IndiaUnclaimed assets across bank deposits, shares, insurance policies and retirement funds have crossed Rs 2 lakh crore. This article explains why this money remains out of reach and what makes it so hard to recover.

Buying a car or bike? The hidden truth behind insurance sold at dealershipsWhen you buy a car or bike, the final bill usually comes as an 'on-road price' that includes insurance. For most buyers, this is accepted without much thought, as it appears to be a standard part of the purchase. But what often goes unnoticed is that this insurance is not mandatory to buy from the dealer.

Middle East war to cut global growth, spike inflation despite truce: World BankThe World Bank's baseline estimate now projects growth in emerging markets and developing economies of 3.65 per cent in 2026, compared to 4 per cent in October, dropping as low as 2.6 per cent in an adverse scenario with a longer-lasting war.

India allows 4 sanctioned tankers carrying Iranian oil to berth at Sikka: ReportIranian oil is often transported by a so-called shadow fleet of vessels that lack internationally recognised insurance and safety certifications. But this requires special permission from the government as exemptions are required under Indian rules for the berthing of ships.

Gold dips, silver slips: Is this the right time to invest or wait?Prices of gold and silver have cooled off after recent highs, but the bigger question remains, is this the right entry point, or is there more downside ahead?

Why April is the best time to reset your budget, SIPs and other investmentsIn Part 2 of our MONEY MAKEOVER series, we explore how April, with salary hikes, bonuses and tax changes, creates the perfect window to pause, reset and move forward with clarity. Let's have a look.

Necessary evolution: SP Group backs Tata Sons listing, seeks RBI clarityIn a statement, Shapoorji Pallonji Mistry said a timely listing is not merely about regulatory compliance, but a step that would reinforce the foundational principles of the Tata Group.

Global shocks may hit every 1–2 years, warns Axis Bank's chief economistAxis Bank chief economist Neelkanth Mishra warns that geopolitical shocks could hit every 1–2 years and explains what India must do to prepare its economy.

Equity inflows jump 55% in March, but debt outflows drag industry negative: AMFIThe data clearly shows that retail investors are becoming more confident and disciplined. While institutional money continues to move in and out of debt funds based on short-term needs, individual investors are steadily increasing their exposure to equities.
Dalal Street records best week in 5 years on Iran-US ceasefire reliefSensex closes 919 points higher, Nifty crosses 24,000; IT stocks tumble

Unclaimed EPF rises to Rs 10,900 crore: Why it's rising and how to claim your moneyYou may not think much about your old PF account after switching jobs, but it could still be holding your money. Unclaimed EPF funds have now crossed Rs 10,900 crore, raising concerns about how easily savings are getting left behind.

TCS shifts focus to AI, infrastructure as demand outlook remains mixedShares of TCS were trading at Rs 2,513 on the BSE, down 2.89% or Rs 74.75, as of 1:27 pm, even as its fourth-quarter performance beat estimates.

RBI wants a 1-hour pause on some UPI payments. Here's whyThe Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has released a discussion paper proposing a one-hour delay for certain digital payments. Here's an explainer on how it could work, which transactions it may affect, and what it means for users.

Sending over Rs 10,000 via UPI? RBI may soon introduce a 1-hour delayIf you often use UPI for bigger payments, a change may be on the way. The RBI is looking at adding a one-hour pause for transfers above Rs 10,000 to help prevent online fraud.

Applying for TAN? Income Tax Dept replaces Form 49B with two new formsApplying for a TAN is set to become a lot simpler. The Income Tax Department has replaced the old Form 49B with two new forms, aiming to make the process clearer and easier for different types of applicants. Let's have a look.

Infosys, TCS, HCLTech: Why IT stocks are down despite market rallyThe fall in IT stocks comes even as the Sensex and Nifty moved higher, showing that the weakness is limited to the sector amid concerns around growth and demand.

Sun Pharma shares fall over 4% amid acquisition concernsSun Pharma shares fell over 3.5%, as investor concerns over a potential mega acquisition outweighed optimism, bringing focus on debt, integration risks and the deal's long-term payoff.

TCS shares down 3%: Why the IT giant's stock fell despite strong Q4 result?TCS shares down: The stock was trading at around Rs 2,515.00, down 2.81% as of 10:15 am. It was also among the top losers on both the Nifty50 and the IT index.

Wipro shares rise as board considers buyback after three yearsWipro has informed exchanges that its board will evaluate a share buyback alongside its fourth-quarter results on April 16, bringing the focus back on shareholder returns.
Sensex ends 900 points higher: 3 reasons why stock market rose todayThe S&P BSE Sensex climbed 687.99 points to 77,319.64, while the NSE Nifty50 added 211.60 points to 23,986.70 as of 9:58 am.

entertainment.headline: Britney Spears hits rock bottom, enters rehab following DUI arrest: Report Britney Spears has reportedly checked into a treatment facility following her DUI arrest in Ventura County. The pop star is said to have entered a voluntary rehab programme amid legal pressure and growing concern from her inner circle.

Video: Asha Bhosle's last public outing was at Arjun Tendulkar's wedding Singer Asha Bhosle's last public appearance was at Arjun Tendulkar's wedding in Mumbai in March. The legendary singer died on April 12, leaving behind a remarkable legacy in Indian music.

Throwback pics show Sonu Nigam washing Asha Bhosle's feet, bowing in respect Sonu Nigam honoured Asha Bhosle's legacy with heartfelt memories and rare photos. He recalled their decades-long journey and remembered their first meeting in 1994.

Katy Perry-Justin Trudeau enjoy at Coachella, her joke on Bieber steals spotlight Katy Perry and Justin Trudeau shared candid moments at Coachella with a playful remark on Justin Bieber's set. The couple's photos and videos from the date night are now taking social media by a storm.

Athiya Shetty trolled for sharing Lata Mangeshkar's pic to mourn Asha Bhosle Athiya Shetty faced social media backlash over sharing Lata Mangeshkar's picture in her tribute to late singer Asha Bhosle.

Justin Bieber draws massive crowds at Coachella, gig falls short of expectations Justin Bieber's Coachella headline attracted a record crowd, but the show did not meet expectations. Observers at the festival reported that the singer rehearsed older tracks during soundchecks, leaving the actual performance lineup uncertain.

Watch: Akshay Kumar's prank leaves Wamiqa Gabbi startled on Bhooth Bangla set Ahead of Bhooth Bangla's release, a behind-the-scenes video from a song shoot has surfaced on social media. In the clip, Akshay Kumar is seen pranking co-star Wamiqa Gabbi.

Shah Rukh Khan, Kajol to Chiranjeevi, celebrities mourn Asha Bhosle's death Actors Shah Rukh Khan, Kajol and Chiranjeevi, among many other celebrities, mourned the death of legendary singer Asha Bhosle. They recalled her timeless voice and legacy with heartfelt posts.

Not just songs, Asha Bhosle's cooking won hearts too; founded restaurant chain Legendary singer Asha Bhosle, who delighted fans with her melodies, was also a passionate cook. Her sister Lata Mangeshkar swore by the shami kebabs Asha Bhosle prepared. This is how, with her signature maa ka daal and biryani, Bhosle won hearts and ran restaurants abroad.
How Asha Bhosle became the queen of Indipop Remembering Asha Bhosle's transformative journey across Indian music genres. Read on to know why the legendary singer was called Queen of Indipop

The last note of the Golden Age: The defiant, dauntless melody of Asha Bhosle Asha Bhosle's journey from living in the shadow of tradition to being the vanguard of a musical rebellion defined an era. With unparalleled range and resilience, she turned the margins of cinema into its heartbeat.
Asha Bhosle's final Instagram post reflects on life, music and spiritual journey Legendary singer Asha Bhosle's last social media message offers a heartfelt reflection on life and music.

10 songs that define Asha Bhosle as a voice for every generation Asha Bhosle's death marks the end of an era in Hindi music. She leaves behind a legacy that shaped generations. Here's a look at ten iconic songs that continue to define her timeless voice and enduring impact.

Abhi na jao chhod kar... Asha Bhosle, voice that refused limits, outlasted every era Asha Bhosle, one of Indian cinema's most versatile voices, has died at 92. Her decades-long journey reshaped playback singing, leaving behind a legacy that continues to echo across generations.

Asha Bhosle, playback icon, dies at 92 in Mumbai Legendary singer Asha Bhosle, 92, died in Mumbai on Sunday. She had been admitted to hospital due to extreme exhaustion and a chest infection, according to her granddaughter Zanai Bhosle.

John Nolan dies at 87: Christopher Nolan remembers actor as first artist he knew Actor John Nolan, known for his work in films like Batman Begins and The Dark Knight Rises, died at 87. His death was confirmed by his nephew, filmmaker Christopher Nolan.

Ranveer Singh vs Ranbir Kapoor: Who is Bollywood's real shape-shifter? The Ranveer Singh-Ranbir Kapoor debate is back, sparked by Dhurandhar: The Revenge and Ramayana Part I. But this time, it goes beyond stardom - into two radically different ideas of what it means to transform on screen.

Deeply concerned: PM Modi prays for Asha Bhosle's recovery amid health scare Prime Minister Narendra Modi said he was deeply concerned about Asha Bhosle's health after her hospitalisation in Mumbai. The 92-year-old singer is under treatment after suffering from extreme exhaustion and chest infection.

Ranveer Allahbadia reacts to Samay Raina's jabs on Still Alive: Kaun Samay? Podcaster Ranveer Allahbadia reacted to comedian Samay Raina's jokes in his comedy special Still Alive. His remarks came amid renewed chatter around the India's Got Latent controversy.

general news.headline: Don't care if they come back or not: Trump shrugs off Iran after talks collapsePresident Donald Trump's remarks came a day after the US and Iran sat for a permanent peace agreement in Islamabad which failed to yield any result.

US to block all maritime traffic to Iranian ports from today after talks fail  Live UpdatesThe US military said the blockade, set to begin on Monday, would be enforced 'impartially against vessels of all nations' accessing Iranian ports and coastal areas, including facilities along the Arabian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman.

Trump's naval blockade gambit after China, Iran expose US economic warfare limitsThe lesson is becoming harder to ignore. In a world where economic interdependence cuts both ways, leverage is no longer the exclusive preserve of any one power. For Washington, the age of unchallenged economic dominance is fading -- replaced by a more contested, unpredictable order where every move carries the risk of unintended consequences.

You'll miss $4 gas: Iran mocks Trump as US moves to blockade shippingIn a post on X, Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf ridiculed the US warning of a naval blockade targeting ships entering or leaving Iranian ports, suggesting the move could backfire economically on American consumers.

Did Trump throw JD Vance under the Iran bus?US Vice President JD Vance, who Donald Trump tasked with getting a deal done with Iran in Islamabad, could be thrown under the bus for not achieving it. Trump, who had earlier joked about blaming Vance for a potential deal failure, and has blamed his colleagues publicly, could place the onus for failed talks squarely on JD Vance's shoulders.

US warships fled Hormuz after IRGC's warning? Iran releases footage of standoffThe episode, captured in footage aired by Iran's state broadcaster IRIB, claimed to show a radio exchange between Iranian naval forces and a US Navy destroyer. The visuals and audio point to a tense exchange at sea, where repeated warnings were issued over open radio channels.

30 Karnataka Congress MLAs make beeline for Delhi, seek Rahul Gandhi meet for berthsAround 30 Karnataka Congress MLAs are heading to Delhi to meet Rahul Gandhi, seeking ministerial berths and pressing for a Cabinet reshuffle. The move follows a strategy meeting in March and comes amid growing demands within the party after recent by-elections, with leaders collectively pushing for inclusion in the ministry.

Trump's ally Victor Orban loses Hungary polls after 16 yearsThe result marks a major setback for nationalist and right-wing allies of both US President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin, with Orban also long seen as Moscow's closest partner inside the European Union.

I followed a case of LPG ghost delivery. This is the scam I foundTalk about the massive raids to recover LPG cylinders from the black market in India. Have you wondered how these make it there? We think we stumbled upon one of the ways in which dealers scam the system: ghost deliveries.

Amity, Ashoka join IITs in shaping the Cognitive Science revolution, driving Artificial IntelligenceCognitive science is, by its very nature, a field that requires the simultaneous presence of psychology, neuroscience, computer science, linguistics and philosophy.

Live: Lebanon death toll tops 2,055 as Israeli offensive continuesUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Talks between Iran and the US faltered on Sunday after US Vice President JD Vance said that Tehran did not accept the US's terms to end the war. Vance left for the US after the talks hit a roadblock. Iran, on the other hand, dubbed the talks a failure and alleged that the US tried to demand excessively. The first phase of US-Iran talks in Islamabad concluded with both sides exchanging written texts to confirm agreements, even as earlier face-to-face meetings marked the start of direct negotiations. Limited progress was reported on key issues, including possible restrictions on strikes in southern Lebanon and movement on unfreezing Iranian assets. Iran has maintained hard red lines, insisting on a Lebanon ceasefire and broader conditions, while tensions remain high with US mine-clearing operations underway in the Strait of Hormuz and warnings issued to American vessels.

This week, we look at why Wi-Fi passes through walls, and the Hollywood actress behind it all.

Trump calls Pope Leo 'weak on crime', attacks his Iran stance in blistering rantDonald Trump launched a blistering personal attack on Pope Leo, calling him "weak on crime" and "terrible for foreign policy" while accusing him of being soft on Iran, and even claiming the pontiff owed his position to Trump's presidency.

Asha Bhosle funeral: End of an era, says Aamir Khan in moving tributeAsha Bhosle Last Rites Live Updates: Legendary Indian singer Asha Bhosle died at 92 in Mumbai on Sunday, April 12, leaving behind an unparalleled legacy of music. Her funeral is set to take place at Shivaji Park at 4 pm on April 13.

Zero lessons earned, was inches away from deal: Iran blames US for talks collapseIran says US 'maximalism' derailed near-final Islamabad MoU as talks collapsed after 21 hours, with tensions escalating amid fresh blockade threats.

Katy Perry-Justin Trudeau enjoy at Coachella, her joke on Bieber steals spotlightKaty Perry and Justin Trudeau shared candid moments at Coachella with a playful remark on Justin Bieber's set. The couple's photos and videos from the date night are now taking social media by a storm.

Indian techie requests flexible hours to care for newborn, US manager's reply is viralAn Indian techie's honest message about wanting flexible working hours to care for his newborn baby was met with warmth by his American manager, emphasising trust and a healthy work culture.

Shooting at US eatery kills 1, dashcam footage shows suspect fleeing with gunA chilling dashcam video has emerged showing a masked, gun-wielding suspect fleeing the scene moments after the mass shooting inside the crowded Chick-fil-A restaurant in New Jersey on Saturday night.

Central India braces for 45°C, Delhi nears its first 40°C day of 2026Central India and the Peninsula are bracing for a sharp 45°C heat surge this week. Delhi is also on track to hit its first 40°C of the season as the cooling effect of rain fades.

Video: Asha Bhosle's last public outing was at Arjun Tendulkar's weddingSinger Asha Bhosle's last public appearance was at Arjun Tendulkar's wedding in Mumbai in March. The legendary singer died on April 12, leaving behind a remarkable legacy in Indian music.

6 killed, 7 injured as bus returning from wedding procession rams truck in UPSix people were killed and seven injured after a bus returning from a wedding procession rammed into a truck. Police rushed to the spot, launched relief operations and shifted victims to hospital.

Most beautiful bride, $1 bn: Uganda army chief's bizarre demand from TurkeyIn a bizarre demand, Uganda's army chief, Muhoozi Kainerugaba, has asked Turkey for $1 billion for its role in Somalia and also demanded that the most beautiful Turkish woman be given to him as his wife. In 2022, Kainerugaba had demanded the hand of Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni in marriage.

sports.headline: One Pandya had to win: Krunal relishing RCB's win over MI on Wankhede homecoming IPL 2026: Krunal Pandya's Wankhede homecoming turned into a statement night as he got the better of brother Hardik. In a high-scoring clash, his control with the ball made the difference for RCB.

Virat Kohli takes an autorickshaw on a Mumbai highway meant for Ferraris IPL 2026, MI vs RCB: Virat Kohli found himself out of sync during a run-fest in Mumbai on Sunday. While his teammates struck at 200-plus, the King's 38-ball fifty left him frustrated.

RCB namma Sinhagulu, Lions of Bengaluru: Vijay Mallya reacts after Wankhede win Vijay Mallya praised RCB after their win over MI at Wankhede, where strong batting performances and key wickets in the middle overs helped secure a comfortable victory.

Hardik Pandya says Mumbai Indians will explore their options after poor start in IPL Hardik Pandya admits MI are struggling to keep up, saying changes will be explored after another defeat, with issues in both batting and bowling hurting their start.

Vaishali suffers untimely loss to leave women's Candidates title race wide open R Vaishali loses to Jiner Zhu in Round 12 as the Women's Candidates 2026 title race opens up, with both players tied at the top. Divya Deshmukh's loss meant that she misses out on the chance to have a podium spot at the Candidates.

Virat Kohli injured? RCB skipper Rajat Patidar first reaction after MI win Rajat Patidar provided an update on Virat Kohli's injury after the RCB star didn't turn up to field for his side during the second innings of the win against MI at the Wankhede stadium on Sunday, April 12.

Salt, Patidar lead RCB's rock and roll show at Wankhede to leave Mumbai reeling Phil Salt and Rajat Patidar's destructive batting and a disciplined bowling performance ensured that RCB returned to winning ways while piling on the pressure on Mumbai Indians. MI struggled to keep up with the momentum in the run-chase despite some late heroics from Sherfane Rutherford.

Manchester City step closer to Arsenal in PL title race, with crushing 3-0 vs Chelsea Manchester City thrash Chelsea 3-0 to boost title hopes, closing gap on Arsenal, while Tottenham sink deeper into relegation trouble after defeat to Sunderland.

More than style of play, Tottenham need to change their mentality: De Zerbi Roberto De Zerbi urges mentality shift at Tottenham after defeat to Sunderland extends winless run to 14 games, leaving Spurs in deep relegation trouble with six matches remaining.

Jannik Sinner beats Carlos Alcaraz in Monte Carlo final to return as World No.1 Jannik Sinner beats Carlos Alcaraz in Monte Carlo final to win his first clay Masters title, reclaim world No.1 and extend his dominant run with a fourth straight Masters 1000 crown.

MI vs RCB: Rohit Sharma walks off injured after pulling his hamstringMI vs RCB, IPL 2026: Rohit Sharma was forced to leave the field after picking up an injury during their chase against the Royal Challengers Bengaluru at the Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai.

IPL 2026: Bengaluru and New Chandigarh emerge frontrunners to host playoffsBengaluru and New Chandigarh have emerged as the frontrunners to host the IPL 2026 playoffs, sources have revealed to India Today on Sunday, April 12.

Virat Kohli throws helmet, gloves in frustration after slow fifty vs MI: VideoIPL 2026, MI vs RCB: Virat Kohli looked frustrated after being dismissed by Hardik Pandya immediately after bringing up a 37-ball fifty against the Mumbai Indians on Sunday at the Wankhede Stadium.

RCB, MI stars pay homage to Asha Bhosle at Wankhede, sport black armbandsMumbai Indians and RCB players paid homage to the legendary Asha Bhosle ahead of their game at the Wankhede stadium on Sunday, April 12. Both teams are wearing black armbands to remember the iconic singer.

Ekana Bhool Bhulaiya: No way out for Rishabh Pant's LSG as Gujarat Titans cruiseLucknow's home ground has turned into a psychological Bhool Bhulaiya for the Super Giants. Every shuffle and strategy seems to lead to another dead end. On Sunday, they suffered a seven-wicket defeat to Gujarat Titans, their seventh loss at home in the last nine outings.

RCB drop Josh Hazlewood for Mumbai match include Jacob DuffyIPL 2026, RCB vs MI: Fast bowler Josh Hazlewood has been dropped as Royal Challengers Bengaluru made a solitary change to their playing XI for the match against Mumbai Indians on Sunday at the Wankhede Stadium.

Let's stick to cricket? GT boss surprises Harsha Bhogle with blunt commentVikram Solanki's blunt on-air reply to Harsha Bhogle during a mid-innings chat grabbed attention, as fans on social media debated whether it was witty, awkward, or simply a call to stick to cricket.

Turning the tables: Pak's Usman Tariq slams Mitchell for using Ashwin's ployUsman Tariq slams Daryl Mitchell for using R Ashwin's suggested tactic to counter his bowling pause, calling it unsporting conduct. The incident happened during a PSL game when Mitchell decided to pull out of the delivery multiple times.

Shubman Gill creates history, breaks Virat Kohli's record with 4000 IPL runsIPL 2026, LSG vs GT: Gujarat Titans' captain Shubman Gill broke Virat Kohli's record to become the youngest batter to complete 4000 runs in the history of the tournament.

Ayush Shetty's dream run ends as Shi Yu Qi wins maiden Badminton Asia titleAyush Shetty's dream run in the Badminton Asia Championships was ended by China's Shi Yu Qi on Sunday, April 12. The World No.2 won in straight games to be crowned the champion of Asia.

KKR receive Matheesha Pathirana boost, pacer set to join squad before CSK clashKKR have received a timely boost ahead of their must-win game against CSK as Matheesha Pathirana is set to join the squad before the clash in Chennai. Pathirana has been out of action since the group stage of the T20 World Cup 2026.

MI vs RCB Highlights: Rutherford 71 in vain as Bengaluru beat Mumbai by 18 runsMumbai (MI) vs Bengaluru (RCB) Highlights, IPL 2026: Sherfane Rutherford scored an unbeaten 71 as the Royal Challengers Bengaluru beat the Mumbai Indians by 18 runs on Sunday at the Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai.

Rishabh Pant trolled for reckless dismissal against GT: Worst batter in white ball cricketIPL 2026, LSG vs GT: Lucknow Super Giants skipper Rishabh Pant scored 18 off 11 balls against Gujarat Titans on Sunday in Lucknow before falling to Mohammed Siraj while attempting a reckless shot.

Yuvraj Singh leads condolences after Asha Bhosle dies: Her voice will live on foreverYuvraj Singh, Harbhajan Singh and others led tributes after Asha Bhosle's passing at 92, as her family shared details of her last rites. An iconic voice across generations, Bhosle's unmatched legacy continues to resonate through her timeless music.

Ayush Shetty vs Shi Yu Qi Highlights: Ayush wins silver, loses final to ShiAyush Shetty vs Shi Yu Qi, Badminton Asia Championships Final Highlights: Ayush Shetty's spirited Badminton Asia Championships runs ends with a silver medal on his neck after losing the men's singles final to World no.2 Shi Yu Qi by 8-21, 10-21.

LSG vs GT Highlights: Gill, Buttler help Gujarat hammer Lucknow by 7 wicketsLucknow (LSG) vs Gujarat (GT) Highlights, IPL 2026: Jos Buttler scored 60 while Shubman Gill made 56 as Gujarat Titans beat the Lucknow Super Giants by seven wickets on Sunday in Lucknow.

BCCI's anti-corruption unit to probe RR manager for using phone in dugoutBCCI's anti-corruption unit will launch an investigation into Rajasthan Royals manager Romi Bhinder after he was found using a phone in the dugout during the match against RCB in Guwahati.

History made: Union Berlin appoint first-ever female coach in BundesligaUnion Berlin have turned to Marie-Louise Eta in a historic move as they fight to stay in the Bundesliga. The first-ever female head coach now has five games to deliver survival and make her mark.

Impossible imagined. Impossible achieved: Inside para-archer Payal Nag's riseLimbless archer Payal Nag's journey from tragedy to world champion is not a miracle but a method, shaped by relentless training and a coach who envisioned what sport had never seen before and built it from scratch.

Gout Gout shatters Usain Bolt's record, runs 200m sprint in 19.67 secondsGout Gout clocked 19.67 seconds to win the 200m at the Australian Championships, becoming the first Australian to break the 20-second barrier. He has also shattered Usain Bolt's U23 timing by a big margin.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
